questions.stem: The book ______ by many students in our school last year.
questions.explanation: 本题考查一般过去时的被动语态。由时间状语'last year'可知要用过去时，且书是'被读'，需用被动语态'was/were + 过去分词'。A是主动语态；B是一般现在时被动；D是现在分词不能作谓语。解题技巧：被动语态的基本结构是'be + done'，先确定时态再确定语态。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—______ you ______ your homework yet?
—Yes, I ______ it an hour ago.
questions.explanation: 本题考查现在完成时与一般过去时的区别。第一空后有yet（已经），是现在完成时的标志词，用Have...finished；第二空后有ago（以前），是一般过去时的标志，用finished。A项第一空错误；C项第二空错误；D项两个空都错误。解题口诀：'yet/already/never/ever'用现完，'ago/yesterday/last year'用过去。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ______ is standing under the tree is my classmate.
questions.explanation: 本题考查定语从句关系代词。先行词是the boy（指人），且关系词在从句中作主语（is standing），故用who。which指物；whom指人但作宾语；whose作定语表所属。解题技巧：先找先行词（人/物），再看从句缺什么成分（主语/宾语/定语）。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Do you think ______ we will have a sports meeting tomorrow?
—I'm not sure. It ______ rain in the afternoon.
questions.explanation: 本题考查宾语从句和情态动词表推测。第一空根据答句'I'm not sure'（不确定）可知应用whether（是否）引导宾语从句；第二空表示不确定的推测用might（可能），must表示肯定推测（一定）。A项第一空缺少'是否'含义；C项第二空must与'not sure'矛盾；D项第一空that无'是否'含义。解题技巧：whether/if表'是否'，不确定用might/could，肯定用must，否定用can't。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: It is ______ for humans to live on Mars without special equipment.
questions.explanation: 本题考查词性转换及前缀。根据常识，没有特殊设备人类不可能在火星生存，im-是否定前缀。A与句意相反；C是副词，is后需接形容词；D是名词。解题技巧：im-/un-/dis-是常见否定前缀，-ble结尾通常是形容词。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We should ______ the traditions of our country and pass them to the next generation.
questions.explanation: 本题考查动词词义辨析。value作动词意为'珍惜、重视'，符合句意'珍惜传统并传承'。discover发现；create创造；doubt怀疑。解题技巧：根据后半句'pass them to the next generation'（传给下一代）可知前半句应是'珍惜'。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: Don't ______ your dreams. Keep trying and you will succeed.
questions.explanation: 本题考查动词短语辨析。give up放弃；look up查阅/抬头看；take up占据/开始从事；put up张贴/搭建。根据'Keep trying'（坚持努力）可知前句是'不要放弃'。解题技巧：熟记常见动词短语，结合上下文逻辑判断。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 本题考查固定搭配。make a difference是固定短语，意为'有影响、起作用'。句意：然而，日常生活中的小行动也能产生重大影响。success成功；progress进步；mistake错误，均不与make构成此搭配。解题技巧：完形填空常考固定搭配，需熟记make a difference, make progress, make a decision等短语。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Many people think that protecting the environment is a big task that only governments can handle. However, small actions in our daily life can also make a great (8)______. Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. We were asked to reduce waste and save resources.

At first, I didn't (9)______ my efforts would matter. But when I saw the results at the end of the month, I was (10)______. By using both sides of paper and turning off lights when leaving classrooms, we saved much energy.

The project taught us that everyone can (11)______ to environmental protection. It doesn't require big changes, just small habits. (12)______ each of us takes responsibility, our planet will become a better place to live.

questions.explanation: 本题考查语境理解。根据下文'But when I saw the results...'（但当我看到结果时）的转折关系，可知前文是'起初我不相信我的努力会有用'。believe相信；hope希望；doubt怀疑（通常用于doubt that...怀疑...不会）；wonder想知道。解题技巧：利用转折连词but判断前后情感相反，后文惊讶说明前文是不相信。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Many people think that protecting the environment is a big task that only governments can handle. However, small actions in our daily life can also make a great (8)______. Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. We were asked to reduce waste and save resources.

At first, I didn't (9)______ my efforts would matter. But when I saw the results at the end of the month, I was (10)______. By using both sides of paper and turning off lights when leaving classrooms, we saved much energy.

The project taught us that everyone can (11)______ to environmental protection. It doesn't require big changes, just small habits. (12)______ each of us takes responsibility, our planet will become a better place to live.

questions.explanation: 本题考查情感线索。前文说'不相信努力有用'，but转折后看到结果，应该是感到'惊讶'。surprised惊讶的；bored无聊的；worried担心的；disappointed失望的。解题技巧：关注文章情感线索，前文否定预期，后文积极结果，形成对比产生惊讶。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Many people think that protecting the environment is a big task that only governments can handle. However, small actions in our daily life can also make a great (8)______. Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. We were asked to reduce waste and save resources.

At first, I didn't (9)______ my efforts would matter. But when I saw the results at the end of the month, I was (10)______. By using both sides of paper and turning off lights when leaving classrooms, we saved much energy.

The project taught us that everyone can (11)______ to environmental protection. It doesn't require big changes, just small habits. (12)______ each of us takes responsibility, our planet will become a better place to live.

questions.explanation: 本题考查动词搭配。contribute to为固定搭配，意为'为...做贡献'。句意：这个项目教会我们每个人都能为环境保护做贡献。give up放弃；look forward to期待；pay attention to注意，均不符合句意。解题技巧：contribute to, pay attention to, look forward to都接to，但含义不同，需根据语境选择。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Many people think that protecting the environment is a big task that only governments can handle. However, small actions in our daily life can also make a great (8)______. Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. We were asked to reduce waste and save resources.

At first, I didn't (9)______ my efforts would matter. But when I saw the results at the end of the month, I was (10)______. By using both sides of paper and turning off lights when leaving classrooms, we saved much energy.

The project taught us that everyone can (11)______ to environmental protection. It doesn't require big changes, just small habits. (12)______ each of us takes responsibility, our planet will become a better place to live.

questions.explanation: 本题考查逻辑关系。句意：如果我们每个人都承担责任，我们的星球将成为更美好的居住地。前后是条件关系，用If引导条件状语从句。Unless除非（=if not）；Although尽管（让步）；Because因为（因果）。解题技巧：主句用将来时（will become），从句用一般现在时（takes），这是if条件句的'主将从现'原则。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Many people think that protecting the environment is a big task that only governments can handle. However, small actions in our daily life can also make a great (8)______. Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. We were asked to reduce waste and save resources.

At first, I didn't (9)______ my efforts would matter. But when I saw the results at the end of the month, I was (10)______. By using both sides of paper and turning off lights when leaving classrooms, we saved much energy.

The project taught us that everyone can (11)______ to environmental protection. It doesn't require big changes, just small habits. (12)______ each of us takes responsibility, our planet will become a better place to live.

questions.stem: According to the study, students who sleep less than 7 hours ______.
options: A. usually get higher test scores
B. can concentrate better in class
C. often feel sleepy after lunch
D. may get lower test scores
questions.explanation: 本题考查细节理解。根据文章第二段'Their test scores were 15% lower than those who slept 8-9 hours'（他们的考试分数比睡8-9小时的学生低15%）可知，睡眠不足7小时的学生可能得到更低的分数。A、B与原文相反；C文中未提及。解题技巧：细节题要精准定位，找到'sleep less than 7 hours'对应段落，对比选项与原文。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Do you often stay up late to finish your homework? Many students believe that sacrificing sleep can help them get better grades. However, recent scientific studies show that this idea is completely wrong.

Researchers from a local university studied 500 middle school students for six months. They found that students who slept less than 7 hours a night had trouble concentrating in class. Their test scores were 15% lower than those who slept 8-9 hours.

"Sleep is like food for the brain," explains Dr. Wang, the lead researcher. "When you sleep, your brain organizes information from the day. Without enough rest, your memory becomes weak, and you cannot learn efficiently (高效地)."

The study also discovered something interesting. Taking a 20-minute nap after lunch can improve afternoon performance by 20%. However, sleeping longer than 30 minutes may make you feel groggy (昏昏沉沉的) instead.

So, if you want to do well in school, don't cut back on sleep. Good sleep habits are not a waste of time—they are the foundation of successful learning.

questions.stem: What can we infer from Dr. Wang's words?
options: A. Students should eat more food before sleeping.
B. Sleeping helps improve memory and learning efficiency.
C. Brain activities stop completely during sleep.
D. Students don't need to organize daily information.
questions.explanation: 本题考查推理判断。根据第三段Dr. Wang的话'When you sleep, your brain organizes information... Without enough rest, your memory becomes weak'可推断，睡眠有助于大脑整理信息，提高记忆力和学习效率。A是对'food'的字面误解；C与'organizes information'矛盾；D与原文相反。解题技巧：推理题要基于原文但不仅限于字面，理解比喻含义（sleep is like food）。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Do you often stay up late to finish your homework? Many students believe that sacrificing sleep can help them get better grades. However, recent scientific studies show that this idea is completely wrong.

Researchers from a local university studied 500 middle school students for six months. They found that students who slept less than 7 hours a night had trouble concentrating in class. Their test scores were 15% lower than those who slept 8-9 hours.

"Sleep is like food for the brain," explains Dr. Wang, the lead researcher. "When you sleep, your brain organizes information from the day. Without enough rest, your memory becomes weak, and you cannot learn efficiently (高效地)."

The study also discovered something interesting. Taking a 20-minute nap after lunch can improve afternoon performance by 20%. However, sleeping longer than 30 minutes may make you feel groggy (昏昏沉沉的) instead.

So, if you want to do well in school, don't cut back on sleep. Good sleep habits are not a waste of time—they are the foundation of successful learning.

questions.stem: The underlined word "sacrificing" in Paragraph 1 probably means ______.
questions.explanation: 本题考查词义猜测。根据上下文'stay up late'（熬夜）和'cut back on sleep'（减少睡眠）可知，sacrificing sleep意为'牺牲睡眠'，即'放弃'睡眠。give up放弃；look for寻找；think about考虑；pay for支付。解题技巧：利用同义复现，后文'cut back on'（削减）与sacrificing同义。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Do you often stay up late to finish your homework? Many students believe that sacrificing sleep can help them get better grades. However, recent scientific studies show that this idea is completely wrong.

Researchers from a local university studied 500 middle school students for six months. They found that students who slept less than 7 hours a night had trouble concentrating in class. Their test scores were 15% lower than those who slept 8-9 hours.

"Sleep is like food for the brain," explains Dr. Wang, the lead researcher. "When you sleep, your brain organizes information from the day. Without enough rest, your memory becomes weak, and you cannot learn efficiently (高效地)."

The study also discovered something interesting. Taking a 20-minute nap after lunch can improve afternoon performance by 20%. However, sleeping longer than 30 minutes may make you feel groggy (昏昏沉沉的) instead.

So, if you want to do well in school, don't cut back on sleep. Good sleep habits are not a waste of time—they are the foundation of successful learning.

questions.stem: What is the best title for the passage?
options: A. How to Finish Homework Quickly
B. The Importance of Sleep for Students
C. Ways to Take a Nap at School
D. Why Students Get Low Grades
questions.explanation: 本题考查主旨大意。文章通过研究结果说明睡眠对学生学习效率和记忆的重要性，呼吁保证充足睡眠。A只是开头提到的现象；C是细节（第四段）；D偏离主题。解题技巧：主旨题要概括全文，找主题句（首段转折后或尾段），本文尾段'Good sleep habits are... foundation of successful learning'点明主旨。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Do you often stay up late to finish your homework? Many students believe that sacrificing sleep can help them get better grades. However, recent scientific studies show that this idea is completely wrong.

Researchers from a local university studied 500 middle school students for six months. They found that students who slept less than 7 hours a night had trouble concentrating in class. Their test scores were 15% lower than those who slept 8-9 hours.

"Sleep is like food for the brain," explains Dr. Wang, the lead researcher. "When you sleep, your brain organizes information from the day. Without enough rest, your memory becomes weak, and you cannot learn efficiently (高效地)."

The study also discovered something interesting. Taking a 20-minute nap after lunch can improve afternoon performance by 20%. However, sleeping longer than 30 minutes may make you feel groggy (昏昏沉沉的) instead.

So, if you want to do well in school, don't cut back on sleep. Good sleep habits are not a waste of time—they are the foundation of successful learning.

options: A. However, with proper planning, 24 hours is actually plenty.
B. In addition, try to finish the hardest task first.
C. As a result, you can focus better on your studies.
D. Then, take a five-minute break every 45 minutes.
E. On the other hand, some students have too much free time.
questions.explanation: 本题考查段落衔接。前句说'许多学生抱怨时间不够'，本句转折指出'然而，如果合理规划，24小时其实很充裕'，However承接上文的抱怨并引出下文的建议。B、C、D是具体建议，不应放在文章开头总起段；E逻辑不符。解题技巧：关注连接词，However表转折，常用于引出一个与普遍认知相反的观点。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students complain that they don't have enough time to finish their homework and hobbies. (17)______ Here are some simple tips to help you make the most of your day.

First, create a to-do list every morning. Write down the three most important tasks. (18)______ This prevents you from wasting time on unimportant things.

Second, remove distractions when studying. Put your phone in another room. (19)______ You will be amazed at how much more you can accomplish.

Finally, don't forget to take breaks. Working for hours without rest actually reduces your efficiency. (20)______ By following these suggestions, you can balance study and relaxation better.

options: A. However, with proper planning, 24 hours is actually plenty.
B. In addition, try to finish the hardest task first.
C. As a result, you can focus better on your studies.
D. Then, take a five-minute break every 45 minutes.
E. On the other hand, some students have too much free time.
questions.explanation: 本题考查逻辑关系（递进）。前句说'写下三件最重要的任务'，本句补充'此外，试着先完成最难的任务'，In addition（此外）表示递进关系，补充说明如何做待办清单。A已用过；C表结果；D谈休息，属于最后一段；E表对比。解题技巧：In addition/Furthermore/Moreover都表示递进，用于补充同类建议。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students complain that they don't have enough time to finish their homework and hobbies. (17)______ Here are some simple tips to help you make the most of your day.

First, create a to-do list every morning. Write down the three most important tasks. (18)______ This prevents you from wasting time on unimportant things.

Second, remove distractions when studying. Put your phone in another room. (19)______ You will be amazed at how much more you can accomplish.

Finally, don't forget to take breaks. Working for hours without rest actually reduces your efficiency. (20)______ By following these suggestions, you can balance study and relaxation better.

options: A. However, with proper planning, 24 hours is actually plenty.
B. In addition, try to finish the hardest task first.
C. As a result, you can focus better on your studies.
D. Then, take a five-minute break every 45 minutes.
E. On the other hand, some students have too much free time.
questions.explanation: 本题考查逻辑关系（因果）。前句'把手机放在另一个房间'，本句'因此，你能更专注于学习'，As a result（因此）表示因果关系，说明前句行为的结果。A、B已用过；D谈休息；E逻辑不符。解题技巧：As a result/Therefore/Thus表结果，前因后果，说明前文动作带来的积极效果。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students complain that they don't have enough time to finish their homework and hobbies. (17)______ Here are some simple tips to help you make the most of your day.

First, create a to-do list every morning. Write down the three most important tasks. (18)______ This prevents you from wasting time on unimportant things.

Second, remove distractions when studying. Put your phone in another room. (19)______ You will be amazed at how much more you can accomplish.

Finally, don't forget to take breaks. Working for hours without rest actually reduces your efficiency. (20)______ By following these suggestions, you can balance study and relaxation better.

options: A. However, with proper planning, 24 hours is actually plenty.
B. In addition, try to finish the hardest task first.
C. As a result, you can focus better on your studies.
D. Then, take a five-minute break every 45 minutes.
E. On the other hand, some students have too much free time.
questions.explanation: 本题考查主题一致性。本段主题是'take breaks'（休息），选项D'Then, take a five-minute break every 45 minutes'（然后，每45分钟休息5分钟）具体说明如何休息，符合段落主题。A、B、C已用过；E与主题无关。解题技巧：选句子填空要关注段落主题句（本段首句），选项必须与段落主题一致。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students complain that they don't have enough time to finish their homework and hobbies. (17)______ Here are some simple tips to help you make the most of your day.

First, create a to-do list every morning. Write down the three most important tasks. (18)______ This prevents you from wasting time on unimportant things.

Second, remove distractions when studying. Put your phone in another room. (19)______ You will be amazed at how much more you can accomplish.

Finally, don't forget to take breaks. Working for hours without rest actually reduces your efficiency. (20)______ By following these suggestions, you can balance study and relaxation better.
